skill.name: lark-task
skill.description: 飞书任务：管理任务和清单。创建待办任务、查看和更新任务状态、拆分子任务、组织任务清单、分配协作成员。当用户需要创建待办事项、查看任务列表、跟踪任务进度、管理项目清单或给他人分配任务时使用。
skill.summary: Lark Task management skill for creating, tracking, and collaborating on tasks and tasklists within the Lark productivity platform.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 252 patterns across 13 files (526 lines), but all findings are FALSE POSITIVES from Markdown documentation. The 'external_commands' are bash examples in code blocks, 'path traversal' patterns are relative doc references (../lark-shared/), and 'weak crypto' detections are not actual cryptographic code. The skill is documentation-only that invokes the lark-cli binary for legitimate task management operations. Minor risk exists from external CLI execution, requiring user confirmation for write operations.

low_findings.title: External CLI Execution
low_findings.description: Skill invokes lark-cli binary for task operations. While the commands are legitimate task management functions, external CLI execution carries inherent risk if the binary is compromised or arguments contain unvalidated user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly requires lark-cli binary and documents CLI commands throughout. This is legitimate functionality but represents an external execution dependency that should be monitored.

content.user_title: Manage tasks and tasklists in Lark
content.value_statement: Streamline your workflow by creating, organizing, and tracking tasks directly within Lark. Collaborate with team members through task assignments, comments, and reminders without leaving your AI assistant.
actual_capabilities: Create tasks with summary, description, assignee, due date, and tasklist association
Update task properties including status, priority, and metadata
Add comments and manage task followers for collaboration
Complete, reopen, or delete tasks based on workflow needs
Create and manage tasklists with multiple tasks and members
Search and filter tasks by completion status, creation time, and due date
limitations: Requires lark-cli binary to be installed and authenticated
Cannot access tasks outside the authenticated user's permissions
API calls require proper scope permissions (task:task:read, task:task:write)
Some operations require user identity rather than app identity

use_cases.title: Personal Task Management
use_cases.description: Create and track daily todos, set reminders for deadlines, and maintain personal productivity without manual app navigation.

use_cases.title: Team Project Coordination
use_cases.description: Assign tasks to team members, track project progress, add comments for status updates, and ensure accountability through shared tasklists.

use_cases.title: Meeting Follow-up Automation
use_cases.description: Quickly create action items during meetings, assign them to attendees, set due dates, and organize them into meeting-specific tasklists.

prompt_templates.title: Create a simple personal task
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a task to remind me to review the quarterly report by Friday

prompt_templates.title: Assign a task to a team member
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a task for John to complete the design mockups, due next Tuesday, and add it to our Sprint Tasks list
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - Delegation with due date and tasklist

prompt_templates.title: Track task progress with comments
prompt_templates.prompt: Find my incomplete tasks due this week, add a comment to each asking for status update, and send reminders to assignees

prompt_templates.title: Create a project tasklist with multiple tasks
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new tasklist called 'Product Launch' and add five tasks: finalize specs, design review, QA testing, marketing prep, and launch coordination. Assign each to the appropriate team members.

output_examples.input: Create a task to buy office supplies
output_examples.output: Task created successfully: 'Buy office supplies' (ID: t_xxx). View at: https://applink.larkoffice.com/client/todo/task/xxxxx

output_examples.input: Show my incomplete tasks due this week
output_examples.output: Found 3 incomplete tasks due this week: 1) Review budget (due: Mar 28), 2) Team sync meeting (due: Mar 29), 3) Submit expense report (due: Mar 30)

output_examples.input: Assign the design review task to Alice and Bob
output_examples.output: Successfully added 2 assignees to 'Design Review'. Current assignees: Alice (ou_xxx), Bob (ou_yyy). Task link: https://applink.larkoffice.com/client/todo/task/xxxxx

best_practices: Always confirm write operations (create, update, delete) with the user before executing CLI commands
Include task URLs in responses so users can quickly navigate to view or edit tasks
Use the schema command to inspect API parameters before making unfamiliar API calls
anti_patterns: Do not create tasks without confirming the summary, assignee, and due date with the user
Avoid using app identity for user-specific operations like get-my-tasks - use user identity instead
Do not guess API parameter structures - always check the schema first

faq.question: How do I get the current user's open_id for assigning tasks?
faq.answer: Run 'lark-cli auth status --json' or 'lark-cli contact +get-user' to retrieve the current logged-in user's open_id, then pass it to the --assignee parameter.

faq.question: Can I search for tasks by name?
faq.answer: Yes, use the +get-my-tasks command with the --query parameter to search tasks by summary. It returns exact matches first, then partial matches.

faq.question: What is the difference between task and tasklist?
faq.answer: A task is an individual todo item. A tasklist is a collection of related tasks (like a project folder). You can organize tasks into tasklists for better management.

faq.question: Do I need special permissions to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, your Lark app needs appropriate scopes: task:task:read for viewing tasks, task:task:write for creating/updating, and task:tasklist:read/write for tasklist operations.

faq.question: Can I assign tasks to multiple people?
faq.answer: Yes, use the +assign command with comma-separated open_ids in the --add parameter to add multiple assignees to a task.

faq.question: How do I handle idempotency for task creation?
faq.answer: Pass the --idempotency-key parameter with a unique client-generated token. If the same key is used, the API returns the existing task instead of creating a duplicate.
